contexts.context: ...it is arguable that English literature haxs been better <mark>served</mark> by its German translators than vice versa.  ...
...to be accessible to all sorts of people, <mark>serve</mark>     intrerestedly and conscientiously in innumerable varied     capacities...
...you, Child of the Old World, in which to <mark>serve</mark> a master was never thought ignoble. Children of...
..., was said to have no flavour but to <mark>serve</mark> as a stimulus or bridge to other flavours....
